name: Intercom
shortDescription: Automate your Intercom support and CRM workspace: manage contacts and companies, run and reply to conversations and tickets, send proactive messages, tag and segment people, submit behavioral events, and author Help Center content - all from a no-code flow.

actions.name: Create Contact
actions.description: Creates a new contact (a User or a Lead) in Intercom. Provide at least an email, an external ID, or a role. Use this to add a person to your workspace before messaging, tagging, or starting a conversation with them.

actions.name: Get Contact
actions.description: Retrieves the full details of a single contact by its Intercom ID. Use this to read a contact's attributes, tags, companies and subscription state before acting on them.

actions.name: Get Contact by External ID
actions.description: Finds a contact by the external_id you assigned when creating it. Use this to look up a contact using your own system's identifier instead of the Intercom ID.

actions.name: Update Contact
actions.description: Updates an existing contact's attributes. Only the fields you set are changed. Use this to enrich or correct a contact's email, name, phone, owner or custom data.

actions.name: Delete Contact
actions.description: Permanently deletes a single contact from Intercom. This cannot be undone. Use Archive Contact instead if you only want to hide the contact from lists.

actions.name: Archive Contact
actions.description: Archives a single contact, hiding them from lists without deleting them. This is reversible with Unarchive Contact. Use this to declutter active lists while keeping the record.

actions.name: Unarchive Contact
actions.description: Restores a previously archived contact, returning them to active lists. Use this to reverse an Archive Contact action.

actions.name: List Contacts
actions.description: Returns a page of contacts from your workspace. Use the cursor to page through results. Use this to browse or export contacts, or to feed a downstream action with contact IDs.

actions.name: Search Contacts
actions.description: Searches contacts by a single attribute filter (e.g. email, name, role or created_at). Use this to find contacts matching a condition before acting on them.

actions.name: Merge Contact
actions.description: Merges a Lead contact into a User contact, combining their data onto the user. The "from" contact must be a lead and the "into" contact must be a user. Use this to deduplicate after a known lead signs up.

actions.name: Create or Update Company
actions.description: Creates a company, or updates it if a company with the same Company ID already exists. Use this single action to keep an account record in sync from your own system, keyed by your external Company ID.

actions.name: Get Company
actions.description: Retrieves a single company by its Intercom ID. Use this to read a company's plan, size, attributes and metadata.

actions.name: Find Company
actions.description: Finds a company by your external Company ID or by name. Use this to locate the Intercom company record matching an account in your own system.

actions.name: Update Company
actions.description: Updates an existing company by its Intercom ID. Only the fields you set are changed. Note the external Company ID itself cannot be changed once set.

actions.name: Delete Company
actions.description: Permanently deletes a company by its Intercom ID. This cannot be undone.

actions.name: List Companies
actions.description: Returns a page of all companies in your workspace. Use this to browse or export companies, or to feed a downstream action with company IDs.

actions.name: Attach Contact to Company
actions.description: Associates a contact with a company, adding the contact to that company's member list. Use this to link a person to the account they belong to.

actions.name: Detach Contact from Company
actions.description: Removes the association between a contact and a company. Use this to unlink a person from an account they no longer belong to.

actions.name: List Company Contacts
actions.description: Lists the contacts attached to a company. Use this to see every person that belongs to an account.

actions.name: Create Conversation
actions.description: Starts a new conversation initiated by a contact (a user or lead). Use this to open a support thread on behalf of a customer. The body is plain text (HTML is not supported).

actions.name: Get Conversation
actions.description: Retrieves a single conversation with its parts (messages, notes, assignments). Use this to read a thread's full history and current state.

actions.name: List Conversations
actions.description: Returns a page of all conversations in your workspace, newest activity first. Use the cursor to page through results.

actions.name: Search Conversations
actions.description: Searches conversations by a single attribute filter (e.g. created_at, state, open). Use this to find threads matching a condition before acting on them.

actions.name: Reply to Conversation
actions.description: Adds a reply to a conversation - a public admin comment, an internal admin note, or a reply on behalf of the user. Optionally attach up to 10 public image URLs (Intercom fetches each one server-side). Use this to respond to a customer or leave a private note for teammates.

actions.name: Assign Conversation
actions.description: Assigns a conversation to an admin or a team. Set the assignee to 0 to unassign. Use this to route a thread to the right owner or inbox.

actions.name: Snooze Conversation
actions.description: Snoozes a conversation until a chosen time, after which it reopens. Use this to defer a thread you do not need to handle right now.

actions.name: Open Conversation
actions.description: Reopens a closed or snoozed conversation. Use this to bring a thread back into the active queue.

actions.name: Close Conversation
actions.description: Closes a conversation, optionally leaving a closing message. Use this to mark a support thread as resolved.

actions.name: Attach Contact to Conversation
actions.description: Adds another contact as a participant on an existing conversation. Use this to loop an additional customer into a shared thread.

actions.name: Convert Conversation to Ticket
actions.description: Converts a conversation into a ticket of the chosen type. Use this to escalate a support chat into a tracked ticket workflow.

actions.name: Create Message
actions.description: Sends an admin-initiated in-app message or email to a user or lead. Use this to proactively reach out to a customer. Email messages also require a subject and a template. Note: the request value for an in-app message is "in_app" but the created message echoes it back in the response as "inapp" (no underscore) - both refer to the same in-app channel.

actions.name: Create Ticket
actions.description: Creates a ticket of the chosen type for a contact, optionally linked to a company and assigned to an admin or team. Use this to open a tracked support or task ticket.

actions.name: Get Ticket
actions.description: Retrieves a single ticket by its Intercom ticket ID. Use this to read a ticket's state, type, attributes and assignment.

actions.name: Update Ticket
actions.description: Updates a ticket's state, assignment, linked company or attributes. Set Open to false to close the ticket. Use this to progress or reassign a ticket.

actions.name: Delete Ticket
actions.description: Permanently deletes a ticket by its Intercom ticket ID. This cannot be undone.

actions.name: Search Tickets
actions.description: Searches tickets by a single attribute filter (e.g. created_at, ticket_state_id, ticket_type_id, open). Tickets have no plain list endpoint, so use this to enumerate them.

actions.name: Reply to Ticket
actions.description: Adds a reply to a ticket - a public admin comment, an internal admin note, or a reply on behalf of the user. Optionally attach up to 10 public image URLs (Intercom fetches each one server-side). Use this to update a customer or leave a private note on the ticket.

actions.name: List Admins
actions.description: Lists all admins (teammates) in your workspace, including their away status. Use this to discover admin IDs for assignment, replies and away-status changes.

actions.name: Get Admin
actions.description: Retrieves a single admin (teammate) by ID, including name, email and away status.

actions.name: Set Admin Away
actions.description: Sets an admin's away status on or off, and whether their new conversation replies should be reassigned. Use this to mark a teammate away (or back) automatically.

actions.name: List Teams
actions.description: Lists all teams in your workspace. Use this to discover team IDs for routing conversations and tickets.

actions.name: Get Team
actions.description: Retrieves a single team by ID, including its name and member admin IDs.

actions.name: Create or Update Tag
actions.description: Creates a tag with the given name, or renames an existing tag if you supply its ID. Use this to manage the tag vocabulary you apply to contacts, companies and conversations.

actions.name: List Tags
actions.description: Lists all tags in your workspace. Use this to discover tag IDs and names for tagging actions.

actions.name: Get Tag
actions.description: Retrieves a single tag by ID, including its name.

actions.name: Delete Tag
actions.description: Permanently deletes a tag from your workspace, removing it from every record it was applied to.

actions.name: Tag Contact
actions.description: Applies an existing tag to a contact. Use this to label or segment a person.

actions.name: Untag Contact
actions.description: Removes a tag from a contact. Use this to unlabel a person.

actions.name: Tag Company
actions.description: Applies a tag (created if it does not exist) to a company. Use this to label or segment an account.

actions.name: Untag Company
actions.description: Removes a tag from a company. Use this to unlabel an account.

actions.name: Tag Conversation
actions.description: Applies a tag to a conversation on behalf of an admin. Use this to categorize a support thread.

actions.name: Untag Conversation
actions.description: Removes a tag from a conversation on behalf of an admin. Use this to uncategorize a support thread.

actions.name: List Segments
actions.description: Lists all contact segments in your workspace, optionally with member counts. Use this to discover segment IDs for filtering and reporting.

actions.name: Get Segment
actions.description: Retrieves a single segment by ID, including its name and person type.

actions.name: Create Note
actions.description: Adds an internal note to a contact, visible only to teammates. Use this to record context about a person that customers should not see.

actions.name: List Notes
actions.description: Lists the internal notes attached to a contact. Use this to read everything teammates have recorded about a person.

actions.name: Get Note
actions.description: Retrieves a single note by ID, including its body and author. Pick the note from the chosen contact's notes.

actions.name: Submit Event
actions.description: Records a custom event for a contact (e.g. "invited-friend"). Events power behavioral targeting and timelines. Identify the contact by your user_id, their Intercom ID, or their email.

actions.name: List Events
actions.description: Lists the recent custom events recorded for a single contact. Identify the contact by your user_id, their Intercom ID, or their email.

actions.name: List Data Attributes
actions.description: Lists the custom and standard data attributes defined for contacts, companies or conversations. Use this to discover the attribute schema before reading or writing custom data.

actions.name: Create Data Attribute
actions.description: Creates a custom data attribute on contacts or companies. Choose its value type; for a List attribute supply the allowed option values. Use this to extend your CRM schema.

actions.name: Update Data Attribute
actions.description: Updates a custom data attribute - archive it, change its description or messenger access, or set new list options. Archiving (Archived = true) is how attributes are removed (there is no delete).

actions.name: Create Article
actions.description: Creates a Help Center article authored by a teammate, as a draft or published. Optionally nest it under a collection. Use this to publish self-serve documentation.

actions.name: Get Article
actions.description: Retrieves a single Help Center article by ID, including its title, state and content.

actions.name: Update Article
actions.description: Updates a Help Center article's title, body, description, state or author. Only the fields you set are changed. Use this to edit or publish documentation.

actions.name: Delete Article
actions.description: Permanently deletes a Help Center article by ID. This cannot be undone.

actions.name: List Articles
actions.description: Returns a page of all Help Center articles. Use the cursor to page through results.

actions.name: Search Articles
actions.description: Full-text searches Help Center articles by phrase, optionally limited to a state. Use this to find documentation matching a query.

actions.name: Create Collection
actions.description: Creates a Help Center collection (a top-level grouping of articles), optionally nested under a parent collection. Use this to organize your knowledge base.

actions.name: Get Collection
actions.description: Retrieves a single Help Center collection by ID, including its name and description.

actions.name: Update Collection
actions.description: Updates a Help Center collection's name, description or parent. Only the fields you set are changed.

actions.name: Delete Collection
actions.description: Permanently deletes a Help Center collection by ID. This cannot be undone.

actions.name: List Collections
actions.description: Returns a page of all Help Center collections. Use the cursor to page through results.

actions.name: List Subscription Types
actions.description: Lists the subscription types (opt-in/opt-out preferences) available in your workspace. Use this to discover subscription IDs before attaching or detaching them on a contact.

actions.name: Attach Subscription
actions.description: Subscribes a contact to a subscription type with an opt-in or opt-out consent. Use this to record a customer's communication preference.

actions.name: Detach Subscription
actions.description: Removes a subscription type from a contact. Use this to clear a previously recorded communication preference.

actions.name: Get Visitor
actions.description: Retrieves an anonymous visitor by your user_id identifier. Use this to read what Intercom knows about an unidentified web/messenger visitor.

actions.name: Update Visitor
actions.description: Updates an anonymous visitor's name or custom attributes, identified by your user_id. Use this to enrich what Intercom knows about an unidentified visitor.

actions.name: Convert Visitor
actions.description: Converts an anonymous visitor into a Lead or User contact, merging into the target contact's user_id. Use this when an unidentified visitor becomes a known person.

idealUseCases: Sync contacts and companies from your own system (create, find, update, merge, archive).
Triage support: create, search, reply to (with optional image attachments), assign, snooze, close, and convert conversations to tickets.
Run tickets end to end - create, update, reply, search, and delete.
Proactively reach customers with in-app or email messages.
Tag and segment contacts, companies, and conversations; record opt-in/opt-out subscriptions.
Track behavior by submitting custom events and listing a contact's event history.
Publish and maintain Help Center articles and collections.
React to new conversations, contacts, or tickets - or to conversations being closed or replied to - with polling triggers.
agentIdeas: When an **Intercom** "New Conversation" trigger fires, use **Slack** "Send Message To Channel" to alert the support team with the conversation details for fast triage.
When a **Shopify** "On New Order" trigger fires, use **Intercom** "Create or Update Company" and "Submit Event" to record the purchase against the customer's Intercom profile.
When an **Intercom** "New Contact" trigger fires, use **Google Sheets** "Add Row" to log each new signup into a tracking spreadsheet.
